title: GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement

compliance.assurance_rationale: GDPR Art.5(1)(e) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.5(1)(e) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.1.4: GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.1.4' running on REST endpoint: /services/data/indexes — fields: title, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, disabled, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required)
spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*", "history", "summary")
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400, 1)
| eval policy_max_days=case(
    match(title,"^(hr|pii|gdpr)"), 180,
    match(title,"^(security|sec)"), 365,
    1=1, 365)
| eval violation=if(retention_days>policy_max_days, "Exceeds_Policy", "OK")
| table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
| sort - retention_days
description: Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for GDPR compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Run from a scheduled search on the search head (requires admin capability for REST); (2) replace the `case()` block with a lookup `index_retention_policy.csv` mapping index names to required max retention days; (3) export results to GRC tickets when violations trigger; (4) pair with archive/freeze path review outside Splunk for complete retention evidence.
visualization: Table (index, retention, policy, violation), Bar chart (retention by index), Single value (violation count).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for audits splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: REST endpoint: `/services/data/indexes` — fields: `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `disabled`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Run from a scheduled search on the search head (requires admin capability for REST); (2) replace the `case()` block with a lookup `index_retention_policy.csv` mapping index names to required max retention days; (3) export results to GRC tickets when violations trigger; (4) pair with archive/freeze path review outside Splunk for complete retention evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*", "history", "summary")
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400, 1)
| eval policy_max_days=case(
    match(title,"^(hr|pii|gdpr)"), 180,
    match(title,"^(security|sec)"), 365,
    1=1, 365)
| eval violation=if(retention_days>policy_max_days, "Exceeds_Policy", "OK")
| table title, retention_days, policy_max_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, violation
| sort - retention_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR Data Retention Policy Enforcement (Art. 5(1)(e))** — Audits Splunk index-level retention settings against written data retention policy so personal data in logs is not kept longer than necessary under the storage limitation principle.

Documented **Data sources**: REST endpoint: `/services/data/indexes` — fields: `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `maxTotalDataSizeMB`, `disabled`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (native `| rest` API, no separate TA required). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **retention_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **policy_max_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **violation** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: frozenTimePeriodInSecs, maxTotalDataSizeMB, policy_max_days, retention_days, title, violation.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (index, retention, policy, violation), Bar chart (retention by index), Single value (violation count).
